description: Grungle Bird cocktail recipes exported from Mixel for database import.

field_notes.amount: string to preserve fractional values like 0.333 and 0.25; '0' means 'to taste' / garnish with no fixed measure
field_notes.unit: measurement unit as authored; empty string means a countable/whole item (e.g. eggs, fruit)
field_notes.name: base ingredient name
field_notes.prep: trailing modifier/brand/spec from the source line, or null
field_notes.category: one of: cocktail, mocktail, punch, syrup, infusion, prep
field_notes.glass: glassware inferred from the Mixel glass icon
field_notes.original: true if a Grungle Bird original creation
field_notes.subtitle: secondary title shown under the name, or null

recipes.name: Allspice Dram

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.prep: add if too thick or too strong

instructions: Toast allspice in heavy pan over low heat for 1 minute, then crush with mortar & pestle.
Add rum and crushed allspice to a mason jar, shake to combine.
Agitate daily for 6 days.
After 6 days, break up cinnamon stick and add to jar.
Continue daily agitation for another 6 days.
After 12 days of infusion, strain through a fine mesh seive, then through a coffee filter.
Add demerara syrup and wait another 2 days before serving.
Total time: 2 weeks.

recipes.name: Bourbon Smash
instructions: Add lemon, mint, and simple to double old fashioned glass. Muddle gently, then and bourbon and ice and stir to chill.

recipes.name: Cinnamon Syrup
instructions: Boil water then add cinnamon sticks and sugar. Return to boil then whisk for 1 minute.
Remove from heat and cover for 12 hours.
Strain through fine mesh & cheesecloth.

recipes.name: Coffee-infused Campari
instructions: Infuse at room temperature 24 hours then strain.

recipes.name: Daiquiri
instructions: Shake well and strain into coupe glass

recipes.name: Fall Back
instructions: Stir with ice, strain and serve up

recipes.name: Gingerbread Flippin' Latte
instructions: Dry shake all ingredients, shake again with ice, double strain, garnish with nutmeg.

recipes.name: Grungle Bird
instructions: Crushed or pebble ice. Open pour.

recipes.name: Hawaiian Sunset
instructions: Serve up

recipes.name: Hot Toddy

ingredients.name: Cinnamon Tea
ingredients.prep: 64 oz boiling water + 10 cinnamon sticks. Combine in crock pot on high, or simmer on low on stove for 60-90+ mins.

instructions: Add lemon, bourbon, honey, angostura to cup. Top with cinnamon tea and garnish with nutmeg.
5:1 honey:water. Wildflower or acacia preferred.
64oz boiling water and 8 cinnamon sticks into crock pot on high, or simmer on stove for 60-90 mins.
If using fresh lemon: cut lemon into 8 wedges. Squeeze 2 wedges per cup and add the spent wedges.

instructions: Shake with ice, strain, serve up

recipes.name: Margarita
instructions: For extra heat, scramble the insides of a jalapeno and add seeds and membrane to the shaker. Shake well and open pour.

recipes.name: Punch: Alton Brown

ingredients.name: tea
ingredients.prep: split between Earl Gray and Hot Cinnamon Sunset

instructions: Steep tea in 208° water for 3 minutes. Remove tea bags.
Add sugar to tea and dissolve, then add port, rum, lemon juice, allspice dram. Cool for 20 minutes at room temp.
Pour milk into large glass container. Slowly pour tea/booze into milk. It will curdle.
Wait at least 1 hour. Curds must settle before moving on.
Place a large double-cheesecloth-lined fine mesh sieve over a large glass container. Slowly pour mixture onto the cheesecloth. It may take a few hours to completely strain.
Chill punch in fridge overnight. Strain through coffee filter next morning. Bottle, store in fridge, serve chilled.

recipes.name: Punch: Break the Fast
instructions: Add to large airtight container: Peels from all lemons; Juice from 3/4 of the lemons; Pineapple chunks (core and cut into chunks); sugar.
Coarsely grind coriander, cardamom, and anise (mortar & pestle or grinder).
Add 5 bags of green tea, ground spices, & cinnamon stick to glass container. Add 24oz boiling water. Steep 5 minutes then remove tea bags.
Pour tea into container with lemon, pineapple, and sugar. Stir, cover, then rest at room temp for 2 hours. Stir halfway through.
Strain mixture into new container then add rum & brandy.

Over medium heat on stove, bring milk to near boil, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Add juice of remaining lemons, then add the alcohol mixture. Gently stir for 1 minute. The milk will curdle. Let cool and rest for 15 minutes.
Slowly strain through a cheesecloth lined mesh strainer. Do not disturb the curds.
Refrigerate overnight to allow for further settling. Strain again into bottle, store in fridge, serve chilled.

recipes.name: Queen's Park Swizzle
instructions: Muddle mint, lime juice, and syrup in glass. Top with rum and bitters. Fill with crushed ice. Mix with swizzle stick or bar spoon.

recipes.name: Ranch Water
instructions: Make in glass over ice

instructions: Shake & strain. Garnish with long thin lemon peel formed into rings with cherry in the center.

recipes.name: Sazerac
instructions: Stir then serve up in rocks glass

recipes.name: Strange Brew
instructions: Shake all ingredients except beer. Strain into beer glass. Top with ice and then beer. Garnish with mint.

recipes.name: Twelve Mile Limit
instructions: Serve up
recipes.source: Vintage Spirits and Forgotten Cocktails (in Smuggler's Cove)

recipes.name: Whiskey Sour
instructions: Dry shake, then shake with ice, then strain into glass. Add drops of bitters to top and drag cocktail pick through to make art

recipes.name: lemon pseudo juice
instructions: Peel lemon, avoiding pith
add lemon peel, sugar, acids, salt to Mason jar, mix
rest at room temp between 2-6 hours, agitating occasionally
combine jar contents and water in blender
pulse until peels are small-fingernail sized
strain through cheesecloth
Will stay fresh up to 3 weeks

recipes.name: lime pseudo juice
instructions: Peel lime, avoiding pith
add lime peel, sugar, acids, salt to Mason jar, mix
rest at room temp between 2-6 hours, agitating occasionally
combine jar contents and water in blender
pulse until peels are small-fingernail sized
strain through cheesecloth
Will stay fresh up to 3 weeks

recipes.name: Ancient Mariner
instructions: Crushed iced open pour

recipes.name: Negroni
instructions: Stir and strain. Can be served with or without ice.

recipes.name: Enzoni
instructions: Muddle grapes in small tin. Add other ingredients to large tin.
Add ice and shake.
Double strain, over fresh ice, into rocks glass. Garnish with one grape

recipes.name: Bloody Mary
instructions: If possible, prepare mix ahead and refrigerate overnight. Rim glass with old bay. Add vodka, mix, and ice to glass and stir.
Otherwise, roll between 2 tins to mix & chill before pouring into glass.
